prompt: A dramatic fictional movie poster for GUGUGAGA, designed like an epic prestige film one-sheet with dark golden-brown cinematic lighting and a serious award-winning tone. The composition is vertical and symmetrical. In the center, a plush black-and-white penguin mascot with a round body, big glossy eyes, a yellow beak, and small yellow feet is held aloft in a ritual-like presentation by multiple African tribal warriors surrounding it from all sides. Exactly 6 visible warriors are arranged around the penguin, with muscular arms reaching inward and upward to support the mascot, creating a radial halo of hands and bodies. Their skin is richly lit with warm highlights, and they wear colorful beaded necklaces, bracelets, headbands, and traditional adornments. The background is a dense, dimly lit ceremonial setting with warm firelight, earthy shadows, fabric banners, and a textured amber glow, giving the scene an ancient, mythic atmosphere. At the very top, centered Japanese copy reads: 「知られざるググガガの誕生秘話。」 On the left side, a vertical Japanese tagline reads: 「モフモフが、世界を変えた。」 On the right side, place exactly 5 stacked gold laurel award blocks with Japanese text, each framed by laurel wreaths. The 5 award texts are: 「第38回 モフモフ オブザイヤー 受賞」, 「アフリカン タッチ映画祭 グランプリ」, 「世界ぬいぐるみ 協会賞 最優秀賞」, 「国際もふもふ アカデミー賞 主演賞」, and 「ぬいぐるみが 選ぶ映画賞 観客賞」. Across the lower middle, place the huge metallic gold serif title GUGUGAGA, with smaller gold serif subtitle beneath it reading THE MOVIE. Below that, add a small centered line reading: STARRING (声の出演). Along the lower section, include exactly 5 circular cast medallions with tiny laurel-like frames and labels beneath them. The 5 cast items are: a fluffy white pomeranian-like dog labeled 「モフリン」, a giraffe labeled 「カワウーソン」, an orange tabby cat labeled 「ニャンバラ」, a blue round toy-like character labeled 「トゥクトゥク星人」, and a scholarly baboon or monkey figure labeled 「バオバブ先生」. Beneath the cast row, add small Japanese-style credit lines including director, screenplay, and music in a fine poster-credit style. At the bottom center, display a large gold release date line reading 7.7 FRI ROADSHOW. Along the bottom edge, include small production and distribution credits reading 「制作：gugugagaTokyo」 and 「配給：AI Studio Tokyo」. Put a black-and-white QR code in the bottom-right corner. Use ultra-detailed photorealistic poster rendering, dramatic contrast, gilded typography, luxurious texture, prestige cinema marketing design, and a tongue-in-cheek parody of an Oscar-bait adventure drama.
